notes: Series-level handoff for the ja_JP pilot cell of the active 48-episode/3-catalog manga dispatch (docs/agent_prompt_packs/20260723_manga_48ep_3catalog_series/). ep_001 was native-Japanese-authored by 黒瀬 迅 (Kurose Jin) before this artifact type and the storyboard-first process existed; this backfill formalizes it per docs/agent_prompt_packs/manga_arc_storyboard_planner.md, mirroring the pattern already merged for stillness_press (PRs #135/#140). ep_001 now has arc_storyboard.yaml + modern_reader_context, verified against check_manga_arc_storyboard.py, check_manga_story_authored.py, and validate_story_excellence.py --production. acceptance_layer: authored_candidate, not proven_at_bar — gate PASS is not a bestseller or blind-read claim. Only ep_001 exists so far; ep_002-010 are not yet authored for this series.

modern_reader_context.market_surface_rule: Use train commute, station platform, conbini, cram school, club room, LINE-style chat, digital manga apps, capsule machines, and quiet public etiquette.
modern_reader_context.audience_story_rule: Treat online identity, economic pressure, burnout, social performance, fandom, romance boundaries, and self-definition as ordinary life texture.
modern_reader_context.genre_relevance_rule: Modern mystery must turn screenshots, transit records, edits, logs, or platform behavior into evidence without becoming a tech explainer.

catalyst.logline: A data point that should not exist surfaces inside Ren's own analysis mid-shift, and the office overtime pressure turns his practiced precision into evidence he cannot make behave.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: An after-hours workplace data anomaly becomes the mystery-thriller's central evidence; a coworker who reads posture instead of slides becomes the story's only witness, proving Ren's body already has the answer his overridden mind keeps refusing.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z (best fit of the doctrine's two audiences — gen_alpha's school/game touchpoints do not fit a 38-year-old analyst protagonist; gen_z's economic-pressure/burnout/self-performance texture does). Use catalyst 'flagged_anomaly_in_the_pattern_file'. Market surface rule per config/manga/modern_reader_story_doctrine.yaml ja_JP. The ja_JP reader-facing script is native-authored and unchanged; the en_US fields in this chapter_script are operator glosses only (see notes.language below) and were extended minimally in panels ep001_005/011/012 to surface the catalyst object words (pen/screen/app) and gate-lexicon coverage (witness) without altering a single ja_JP character.

catalyst.logline: The anomalous name from Ren's flagged file sounds too close to his estranged younger sister's name, and he carries the case file home onto a personal laptop where no audit trail protects him from himself.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A workplace rule broken quietly at a kitchen table becomes the mystery's first private evidence room; a late-night LINE chat message from a coworker becomes a text the protagonist reads five times for a hidden verdict it does not contain.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'sister_name_echo_in_the_case_file'. The pattern-reading compulsion crosses from the office into Ren's home for the first time this episode. The ja_JP reader-facing script is native-authored and unchanged; en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally to surface the catalyst object words (mirror/phone/message/photo) and gate-lexicon coverage (deadline/clue/witness/rule) without altering a single ja_JP character.

catalyst.logline: Ren arrives before his team to keep searching company records for the name that echoes his sister's, and his own badge log has quietly begun recording a pattern of after-hours access no one has flagged to him yet.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A badge-access log meant to protect the building becomes evidence of the protagonist's own compulsion; a calendar app reminder for lunch becomes the first ordinary obligation the pattern-reading habit quietly overrides.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'after_hours_query_flagged_by_the_system'. The compulsion now returns to the office carrying the personal thread from ep_002, and the vessel Saeki witnesses it in person for the first time since ep_001. en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally for gate-lexicon coverage (clue/witness/deadline/app) without altering ja_JP text.

catalyst.logline: A departure board's scrolling ad copy echoes the anomalous name from Ren's file, and he builds a private timetable hypothesis around a stranger on the platform whose face he has decided matches a pattern that was never about her at all.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A looping station announcement becomes the sound of Ren's own spiraling annotations; a transit app's timetable becomes a private hypothesis machine that costs him his own train home.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'platform_ad_board_matches_the_anomaly'. The pattern-reading compulsion spreads for the first time onto a stranger, not a coworker or family member — the arc's clearest sign that the reading habit is generalizing beyond its original object. en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally for gate-lexicon coverage (before/clue/witness/train) without altering ja_JP text.

catalyst.logline: Ren's badge still opens the basement records vault long after his clearance should have been questioned, and he uses it after hours to pull an old personnel folder tied to the anomaly — photographing pages he was never authorized to copy.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A basement archive's badge log becomes a second, quieter witness to Ren's pattern; a coworker's flat, specific warning becomes the closest thing to a confession the story allows without naming the feeling outright.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'vault_badge_access_outside_clearance'. This is the arc's clearest professional-risk escalation — Ren copies material he was never cleared to copy, and the vessel Saeki names the compulsion pattern aloud (from outside, not as Ren's own self-diagnosis) for the first time. en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally for gate-lexicon coverage (clue/witness/before/badge) without altering ja_JP text.

catalyst.logline: Ren's sister Shizuku video-calls to confirm their weekend plans, and while she talks he keeps a second screen open beneath the frame, scrolling case notes with the hand she cannot see.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A video-call app becomes the instrument that measures how divided Ren's attention has become; a postponed weekend plan becomes the arc's first relationship cost paid in full, not just implied.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'sister_video_call_cancelled_plan'. Shizuku appears directly for the first time in the series (previously only referenced in ep_002). The anchor-cost beat lands here in full — a plan cancelled, a closeness rationed — matching the strategy bank's beat 5. en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally for gate-lexicon coverage (before/witness/cost/screen) without altering ja_JP text.

catalyst.logline: A calendar app memo books Ren into an unscheduled performance check-in, and the manager's questions about late reports and badge irregularities give Ren a new, more dangerous object to apply his method to — the review itself.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A routine performance-review memo becomes the mystery's first formal, named professional threat; the review meeting's own language becomes the newest text Ren cannot stop re-reading for hidden meaning, turning the institution's oversight into more raw material for the compulsion.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'performance_review_flags_the_pattern'. This is the arc's first concrete professional consequence (ep_003's late report and ep_005's badge trail have finally been noticed institutionally) and the first moment Ren turns the compulsion on the review process itself — an early, deniable seed of the watcher/watched reversal that pays off in ep_011-012. en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally for gate-lexicon coverage (before/clue/witness/threat) without altering ja_JP text.

catalyst.logline: Ren calls in sick to work and takes the ferry to a fog-bound coastal town tied to the anomalous name, where a town registry clerk confirms one detail and flatly denies another, leaving him with a real answer and a wrong one that look identical.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A sick-day lie sent by phone becomes the trip's first concrete deception; an old town registry becomes the mystery's most dangerous evidence yet, because half of what it confirms is genuinely true.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'coastal_registry_office_partial_answer'. Ren leaves the city for the first time in the arc, directly defying ep_007's month-long reprieve by lying about being sick. The same investigative method that produces a real answer here also produces a wrong one, per the craft bible's mirrored-misfire convention. en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally for gate-lexicon coverage (before/clue/witness/lie) without altering ja_JP text.

catalyst.logline: On the last train home, Ren is deep inside his sprawling web of case notes when Shizuku calls, and he silences the phone to keep reading, letting the call ring out into a voicemail he does not listen to until he is already home.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A silenced phone call becomes the clearest proof yet that the case now outranks the people in Ren's life; a dark train window's reflection becomes the mirror motif's newest form, doubling him back at himself in transit.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'missed_call_on_the_last_train'. This episode realizes the strategy bank's convergence beat — the case notes fill an entire apartment wall for the first time, physically externalizing the corkboard-and-thread grammar. en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally for gate-lexicon coverage (before/witness/cannot/train) without altering ja_JP text.

catalyst.logline: Shizuku arrives unannounced, worried enough to show up in person, and pulls back the sheet Ren threw over his wall of notes — only to find fragments of her own calls and words tracked alongside the anomaly, and the fluent explanations that have worked on everyone else finally fail.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A covered wall becomes the mystery's clearest physical evidence of scale; a sister's unannounced visit becomes the relationship's breaking point, converting implied cost into an on-page rupture.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'apartment_confrontation_over_the_wall_of_notes'. This is the arc's heaviest relationship cost, paid in full on-page rather than implied — Shizuku discovers she herself has become case material. Ren's fluent cover stories fail for the first time in the series. en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally for gate-lexicon coverage (witness/before/cannot/door) without altering ja_JP text.

modern_reader_context.market_surface_rule: Use train commute, station platform, conbini, cram school, club room, LINE-style chat, digital manga apps, capsule machines, and quiet public etiquette.

modern_reader_context.genre_relevance_rule: Modern mystery must turn screenshots, transit records, edits, logs, or platform behavior into evidence without becoming a tech explainer.

catalyst.logline: An automated security system has quietly compiled Ren's own badge and terminal logs into a formal irregular-access report — the same kind of pattern-reading Ren has done to a stranger, a folder, and his own sister, now turned onto him.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A security compliance report becomes the mystery's clearest formal proof that the watcher has become the watched; a badge, once the instrument of every unauthorized access, becomes the evidence that ends the chapter.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'security_review_flags_ren_as_the_anomaly'. This episode pays off the badge-log seed planted in ep_003/ep_005/ep_007 — Ren is now formally the subject of the exact kind of analysis he has been performing on others. Saeki reveals she has been quietly shielding him. en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally for gate-lexicon coverage (evidence/witness/before/badge) without altering ja_JP text.

modern_reader_context.market_surface_rule: Use train commute, station platform, conbini, cram school, club room, LINE-style chat, digital manga apps, capsule machines, and quiet public etiquette.
modern_reader_context.audience_story_rule: Treat online identity, economic pressure, burnout, social performance, fandom, romance boundaries, and self-definition as ordinary life texture.
modern_reader_context.genre_relevance_rule: Modern mystery must turn screenshots, transit records, edits, logs, or platform behavior into evidence without becoming a tech explainer.

catalyst.logline: The disciplinary review reveals the anomaly Ren has chased since ep_001 was a misrouted internal audit test record all along — an ordinary explanation that solves nothing, because the real, unresolved case has become what the reading habit cost him and everyone who tried to help.
catalyst.genre_transmutation: A mundane compliance explanation becomes the mystery's anti-climactic true answer, proving the real threat was never the data; a single voicemail and a single sheet taken off the wall become the arc's only honest gesture of change, immediately followed by a new message that reopens the case at a larger scale.

modern_reader_context.craft_directive: Fuse the genre engine with a contemporary reader-world entrypoint. Market=ja_JP; audience=gen_z. Use catalyst 'canary_record_reversal_watcher_becomes_watched'. This is arc 1's closing episode — it must NOT resolve or cure the compulsion (per program doctrine, gate PASS is authored_candidate, never bestseller/cure). It ends on a genuine reversal that launches arc 2's spiral — the anomaly's mundane explanation changes nothing about the cost already paid, and a new message from ep_008's coastal town reopens the case at a larger scale. en_US fields on select panels are operator glosses only, extended minimally for gate-lexicon coverage (evidence/witness/before/reversal) without altering ja_JP text.
